scope: Full legacy-parity migration: all 7 legacy read streams (sites, sitemaps, and the 5 search_analytics_by_* dimension views). Legacy is read-only (no reverse-ETL write path); the full Search Console API surface (sitemap submit/delete, URL inspection) is out of scope until Pass B capability expansion. The 5 search_analytics_by_* entries below are declared method GET (not the literal wire HTTP verb, which is always POST for searchAnalytics.query) to correctly signal READ semantics to connectorgen validate's capabilities.write/read cross-check (design section E.1 rule 4; monday's api_surface.json documents the identical convention for its GraphQL POST-only transport) -- Search Console's searchAnalytics.query uses POST to carry a request body for what is semantically a read (a report query), never a mutation.

excluded.reason: sitemap deletion is a destructive admin action, not a syncable read/write record
adding a site property requires site-owner-level scope beyond a read-only connector
removing a site property is a destructive admin action, not a syncable record
